title: The Slow Mirror
summary: Ben buys an old face thing from a shop on Saturday.
coverScene: A cozy bedroom at night with a large, beautifully designed wooden mirror reflecting a young man who is standing perfectly still.

sentences.text: Ben had always loved old things, which was why he spent his Saturdays exploring the dusty shops in town.
One rainy afternoon, he discovered a beautiful mirror with a heavy wooden frame that was designed with strange patterns.
The glass was perfect, showing no signs of age, and it seemed to shine even in the dark corner of the shop.
"If I had more money, I would buy that whole table too," Ben whispered to himself, admiring the fine craft.
However, the mirror was surprisingly cheap, so he paid the owner immediately and carried his new possession home.
He hung it on the wall opposite his bed, where it quickly made the entire bedroom look much larger.
That night, as Ben was brushing his teeth before bed, he looked into the mirror to check his reflection.
As he raised his hand to wipe his mouth, he felt a strange feeling, like a cold wind passing.
He stared at his image, and for a brief moment, he thought he saw a slight delay in his movement.
It was as if the person in the glass was reacting just a tiny bit after him.
He shook his head, laughing quietly at his own imagination, and decided that he was simply tired from work.
If he had gone to sleep earlier, he would not have imagined such a silly thing.
The next morning, the delay was no longer a trick of his tired mind, but a clear reality.
When Ben waved his hand, the reflection waited until he had completely finished the movement before it began to wave.
He stood frozen in front of the glass, watching his own face smile back at him three seconds late.
It was a deeply disturbing sight, yet he was also fascinated by this impossible physical phenomenon.
He spent an hour testing the mirror, walking back and forth, and each time the reflection followed slowly behind.
Although he wanted to tell his friends about it, he was afraid that they would think he was crazy.
During the following week, the delay between Ben and his reflection grew longer and more obvious.
By Wednesday, the image in the mirror was nearly ten seconds behind his actual physical actions.
He would sit at his desk to read a book, and the reflection would still be standing near the door.
The mirror was slowly becoming a window into a past moment, which made Ben feel very uncomfortable in his bedroom.
He tried to cover the glass with a thick sheet, but he felt an odd urge to keep watching it.
It was like a game that he could not stop playing, even though the stakes felt increasingly dangerous.
One evening, Ben noticed that the reflection was not just slow, but it also seemed to have a different attitude.
While Ben was looking down at his phone, he felt the distinct feeling of being watched by his own eyes.
He quickly looked up, but the reflection was still busy looking down at the phone in the glass.
A few seconds later, the reflection finally raised its head, and its eyes locked onto Ben with a cold stare.
The smile on the reflected face was slightly wider than any smile Ben had ever given in his life.
If he could have returned the mirror to the shop, he would have done so immediately, but the shop was closed.
That night, a terrible storm began, and the sound of heavy rain beat loudly against his bedroom window.
Ben lay in bed, unable to sleep, staring at the dark frame of the mirror across the quiet room.
The pale light from the street lights outside cast long, moving shadows that danced across the wooden floor.
Suddenly, he decided to end this mystery once and for all by breaking the mirror with a heavy chair.
He stood up from his bed, walked slowly to the center of the room, and stopped directly in front of it.
The reflection was still lying in the bed, its eyes closed, completely unaware that Ben had moved.
Ben stood perfectly still, holding his breath, waiting to see how long it would take for his image to catch up.
After ten seconds, the reflection in the mirror slowly opened its eyes and sat up on the edge of the bed.
It looked around the reflected room, and then it turned its head to look directly at the real Ben standing there.
Instead of walking forward to match Ben’s position, the reflection did something that made Ben’s heart freeze with absolute fear.
It stood up, but it did not walk toward the glass; instead, it turned its back and walked toward the door.
Ben tried to move his arms, but he realized that his own body was completely frozen, unable to make any sound.
He watched with fear as his reflection opened the bedroom door in the mirror and stepped out into the dark hall.
As the reflection left the room, the lights in the real bedroom began to fade into a deep, heavy darkness.
Ben was left standing alone in the cold silence, unable to turn his head or even close his burning eyes.
He realized then that he was no longer the person who controlled his own physical life in the real world.
He was now just a trapped image, waiting forever for a master who would never return to the glass.
If only he had never bought that beautiful, terrible mirror, he would still be free in his own home.

quiz.q: Why did Ben buy the mirror in the first place?
options: It was surprisingly cheap and had a beautiful design.
The shop owner forced him to take it.
He hoped it would help him sleep better at night.

quiz.q: How did Ben first notice that something was wrong with his reflection?
options: The reflection spoke to him in a loud voice.
There was a slight delay in his reflection's movements.
The reflection had a completely different face.

quiz.q: What did the reflection do at the end of the story?
options: It broke the glass and entered the real bedroom.
It pulled Ben into the mirror with a strong hand.
It walked out of the room, leaving Ben frozen in the real world.

sentences.text: Ben liked to find old things for his house.
He visited small shops on every Saturday.
One day, he found a very beautiful thing.
It was a thing to see your own face.
It had a big part around it with different shapes.
The front part was very good and clean.
The price was very low.
Ben paid for it and he went home.
He put the thing on his sleep room wall.
The room looked very large now.
That night, Ben went to wash his face.
He looked into the new thing on the wall.
He moved his right hand to his face.
He felt a cold wind on his body.
For a moment, he saw a movement.
The movement was not fast at all.
The image in the thing was late.
Ben laughed at himself in the room.
He needed to sleep after his hard work.
He needed to sleep for many hours.
The next morning, the wait was real.
Ben moved his left hand this time.
The image in the thing waited for three seconds.
Then the image began to move too.
Ben stood still and watched his own face.
It was a very different thing to see.
He felt fear but he also had much interest.
He did not tell his friends about the thing.
They would think he was not well.
The image became even more late during the week.
By Wednesday, the image was ten seconds late.
Ben sat down and he read a long book.
The image still stood by the door in the thing.
The thing showed what happened in the past.
Ben felt very worried about this problem.
He tried to cover the front of the thing.
But he wanted to keep watching the image.
One evening, the image changed its behavior.
Ben looked down at the screen of his phone.
He felt that someone watched him from the wall.
He looked up at the thing very quickly.
The image still looked down at its own phone.
Then the image raised its head to look at Ben.
It had a cold look in its eyes.
Its smile was too wide.
Ben wanted to return the thing.
But the shop was closed for the night.
A lot of water fell from the air that night.
Ben did not sleep at all that night.
He looked at the thing in the dark room.
Dark shapes moved across the floor.
Ben decided that he must break the thing.
He took a big object with much weight.
He walked to the room center.
The image still lay in the bed in the thing.
Ben stood still and he waited for the image.
Ten seconds later, the image opened its eyes.
It sat up on the bed in the thing.
It looked at the real Ben in the room.
Then the image did something that was very bad.
It did not walk toward Ben.
It turned and walked toward the door.
Ben tried to move his arms and legs.
He was still and could not speak.
His image opened the door and it left.
The lights in the sleep room became very dark.
Ben was alone in the place with no sound.
He could not move his head or his body.
He was now just an image in the thing.
He could never leave that place again.
The other person was now free.
Ben was sorry that he bought that thing.

quiz.q: Why was Ben happy with the mirror at first?
options: It was very cheap.
It was very new.
It was very small.

quiz.q: What was the problem with the image in the mirror?
options: It was the wrong color.
It was too slow.
It was too dark.

quiz.q: What did the image do at the end?
options: It broke the mirror.
It stayed in the bed.
It walked out of the room.
